title: rumex acetosa subsp. islandicus
intro: Rumex acetosa subsp. islandicus, also known as Icelandic sorrel, is a subspecies of the common sorrel plant that is native to Iceland. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves, which are rich in vitamins A and C, as well as minerals such as iron and calcium. The medicinal properties of Rumex acetosa subsp. islandicus include its ability to act as an astringent and diuretic, helping to reduce inflammation and promote digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and provide health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
